reasoning: The market resolves YES if the WHO declares a new COVID pandemic (other than SARS-CoV-2) by December 31, 2025. The background relies on a sensationalized February 2025 finding of a bat coronavirus (HKU5-CoV-2) with the potential to infect human cells. However, multiple recent articles (up to June 2025) confirm there have been absolutely zero reported human cases of this virus, and no other novel coronavirus outbreaks have emerged in humans. For an entirely new zoonotic virus to jump to humans, sustain human-to-human transmission, spread globally, and trigger an official WHO pandemic declaration within the remaining four months of 2025 is exceedingly unlikely. The historical timeline for pandemic recognition (from zero known human cases) typically spans many months or years. Even the highly transmissible SARS-CoV-2 took over three months from its first case to reach pandemic status. Thus, the real-world probability is indistinguishable from the baseline risk of any novel pandemic emerging and being declared within a 4-month window, which is well under 1-2%.
